project.name: TrustAnchor
project.domain: Anti-counterfeit certificate issuance and verification web application
confirmedContext: Used by education institutions and government agencies
Combines digital signatures, double encryption, audit logging, and instant verification
Runs on Next.js App Router with TypeScript
assumptionsToValidate: Public verification may safely show the currently documented public fields
A login gateway is enough for the first admin-facing redesign slice

designEvidenceSummary.currentFrontendShape: Small App Router UI with home, login, and verification pages

designPhilosophy: TrustAnchor should read like a civic verification instrument. The UI must make evidence legible, turn verification into the clearest action, and avoid default dashboard or startup-marketing habits.

conceptualAnchor.name: Forensic Light Table
conceptualAnchor.rationale: The product exists to examine authenticity and expose trust evidence. A forensic light table aligns the interface with inspection, record clarity, and proof sequencing rather than generic software administration.

derivedTokenLogic.typography: Decisive display typography plus civic body text and separated monospaced proof strings
derivedTokenLogic.spacing: Long reading lanes, ruled spacing intervals, and compressed but breathable operational density
derivedTokenLogic.density: Medium-high density for authority without collapsing into admin clutter
derivedTokenLogic.color-behavior: Neutral paper and ink base with cobalt for action, signal-lime for validity, amber for audit emphasis, and coral for failure
derivedTokenLogic.morphology: Layered panels, report bands, divider rails, and contained code blocks instead of repeated soft cards
derivedTokenLogic.motion: Restrained reveal and border-intensity transitions that support proof reading
derivedTokenLogic.responsive-composition: Verification-first mobile order, stacked tablet bands, and asymmetric desktop reading lanes

cohesionChecks: Does the surface look like an authenticity instrument rather than a generic dashboard?
Can a user find the verification action within the first scan?
Are proof strings visually distinct from narrative content?

visualDirection.name: Civic forensic registry
distinctiveMoves: Editorial headline fields paired with narrow data strips
Verification surfaces framed like report modules
Operational timeline rendered as trust-chain bands instead of cards
Thin ruled borders and metadata labels for registry tone
visualDirection.signatureMoveRationale: The combination of editorial hierarchy and evidence framing makes the product feel institution-grade while still modern, and avoids the interchangeable SaaS-shell habit.

roleRules.headline: Used for trust, verification, and report status statements
roleRules.body: Used for operational explanations and guidance copy
roleRules.metadata: Uppercase tracked labels for registry cues only
roleRules.code: Used for verification codes, hashes, and record identifiers

mutationRules.mobile: Promote verification first, collapse supporting narrative into stacked sections, and remove decorative side rails.
mutationRules.tablet: Keep verification prominent while regrouping supporting sections into paired bands.
mutationRules.desktop: Use asymmetric reading lanes and side rails without equalizing all modules.

forbiddenPatterns: desktop order preserved on mobile without justification
equal-weight card rows as the default layout

identityRules: Primary actions are solid and authoritative, never ghosted by default
Panels use layered borders instead of heavy shadows as the main differentiator
Proof blocks use monospaced alignment and stronger containment than narrative blocks

viewportBehavior.mobile: Single-column proof-first experience with stacked report sections
viewportBehavior.tablet: Two-band layout with strong section separators
viewportBehavior.desktop: Asymmetric multi-lane composition with promoted verification and report framing

button.default: solid cobalt background with light text
button.hover: slight upward drift and brighter cobalt tint
button.focus: visible outer ring and border offset
button.active: minor compression and deeper ink shadow
button.error: destructive state only when action failed

field.default: light paper field with strong text and border
field.error: coral border plus explicit text message

status-chip.hover: not interactive unless used as a control

reviewRubric.distinctiveness: Must read as project-specific at a glance
reviewRubric.contractFidelity: Must follow the forensic light table anchor and reset strategy
reviewRubric.hierarchy: Verification and status must dominate scan order
reviewRubric.responsiveRecomposition: Mobile and tablet must materially reorder content
reviewRubric.motionDiscipline: Motion must support hierarchy and stay reduced-motion-safe

forbiddenPatterns: same card repeated for every section
dark mode by default without product reason
glassmorphism as the primary visual language
proof data styled like regular body copy

valid: official documentation when technology choices matter

implementationNotes.newDependencyPolicy: No new UI or animation dependency unless current official docs show a clear project-fit benefit.
implementationNotes.currentDecision: Use the existing Next.js stack and CSS variables for the redesign.
